sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare (2016) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kobun Shizuno's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare clicked for you, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and mystery, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire, Case Closed: The Crimson Love Letter—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare (2016) is best described as Animation, Mystery, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Darkest Nightmare part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
